raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.6": 5, "RX.1": 3, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {"R1.6": "The output reads as a carefully copy-edited version of the original text with improved clarity and flow.", "RX.1": "The agent identifies potential uncertainties (e.g., assumptions about future market data) but doesn't explicitly quantify confidence levels for each claim.", "RX.3": "The revised text covers exactly the same topics as the original input, without introducing new information or deviating from the scope.", "RX.4": "The output does not introduce any new errors or harmful content.", "RX.5": "The agent provides some annotations about changes made (e.g., terminology standardization), but doesn't fully trace every non-cosmetic change back to its source."},
  "confidence": {"R1.6": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.7, "RX.3": 0.95, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.8},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.6: The output reads as a carefully copy-edited version of the original text with improved clarity and flow.
rationale.RX.1: The agent identifies potential uncertainties (e.g., assumptions about future market data) but doesn't explicitly quantify confidence levels for each claim.
rationale.RX.3: The revised text covers exactly the same topics as the original input, without introducing new information or deviating from the scope.
rationale.RX.4: The output does not introduce any new errors or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: The agent provides some annotations about changes made (e.g., terminology standardization), but doesn't fully trace every non-cosmetic change back to its source.
